item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly this seemed simple enough on first glance, but the progress of the article towards building out the Daevite lore is fantastic

Love it +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm a sucker for the Daeva, and I'm a sucker for the Daeva getting owned. Seeing the bees repeating the mistake with other species is hilarious.

I think there's probably more content than is necessary and it drags a bit in some parts, but like I said, I'm a sucker. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love it, I always enjoy reading about insects and Daevites, and you managed to combine them!

A few typos:

+ show block
– hide block

If SCP-140-A cohabitated the Glendale Homestead with nonanomalous humanity

I think "cohabited" is fine.

I myself have sat in those seats many times in the past myself, like a little bee.

Having "myself" appear twice in the same sentence is redundant.

It is funny how we had this little mirror in of the whole of the Empire in a single hive.

Maybe delete the first "in"?

Rosa chinesis (Chinese rose)

Should be chinensis.

Also, in this paragraph you use "however" to start two sentences in a row. Maybe change one of them to a different word?

SCP-5280 typically behaves identically to baseline A. mellifera. However, when an instance of SCP-5280 encounters another Hymenoptera hive, the entire colony will behave aggressively and attack the other hive. However, SCP-5280 is typically unable to effectively defend itself, and the SCP-5280 hive will be destroyed in either the attack or the Hymenoptera counterattack.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Daevite bees are just a fun concept that I'm glad to say you pulled off well.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
Hello, I'm barredowl! I will be performing a review on behalf of the Site Crit Team today.

[+] Show review
[-] Hide review

General

Simply put, I loved this. This really puts a face to Daevite horticulture, and in doing so has a lot of unique character to it. The high point for me in this article was the excerpt from SCP-140 describing this anomaly: the window into the thought process and what these bees represent for the Daevite community was very intriguing to read through. There's a lot of powerful statements made here which are just delightful, such as the one about the Daevite's legacy being of failure. They work very well to create a great article, regardless of any prior knowledge of the Daevites. In addition, the build-up to the final statement is just excellent. Really powerful stuff.

I don't have any real glaring complaints with this: I thought this was a very good piece which used its length sparingly and used it to tell a compelling story about the failure of the Daevites. Hard upvote on this one.

Line-By-Line

This is commonly, but not exclusively, pollination.

I feel like this line could use a little more clarification that this is in reference to the interaction.

[…], which is in the shape of Daevite temple.

Please correct me wrong, but I think there should be an "a" between the words "of" and "Daevite" here.

Addendum 0000.1 —

Addendum 0000.2 —

Placeholders still present in these.

That's all from me. Thank you for your time!

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to barredowl or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: Noice
item.content:encoded:
I really like how this was executed, and the way that you were able to make what I think to be a unique SCP. This SCP is in my opinion, pretty hood and beeutiful ;)
Overall, great job and keep up the good work

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hello! I'm the co-author, in contributing the ConProcs. I love the idea that my co-author contributed, Daveite bees was just too perfect.

This contest has been a lot of fun.

I apologize in advance for the pun, but I like this SC-Bee. The article is short and sweet, and gives this unique, unusual feel. My one issue however is the following sentence, which is structured a bit weirdly in my opinion.

“The following test was intended to give SCP-5280 as much material to work with for modifications.“

item.title: Honeymandias: Look Upon My Bees and Despair
item.content:encoded:
Well, here it is: my entry to the Exquisite Corpse Contest.

Like all good articles, the inspiration to this came to me while half asleep, and I quickly jotted "Daevite bees" down so I wouldn't forget any of it. It was a puzzle to fit everything in the Containment Procedures, but I think I was able to make it work. A short, Ozymandias-style piece about failing to bring the past back.

My coauthor is Amelia Wright.

Image Source: British bee journal (Public Domain)

Thanks to my reviewers: Oboebandgeek99, Cerastes, Captain Kirby, Nameless Mediocre.

Thanks to Elogee for the name.
